steps.title: State the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: Relationship: substitution/contrast. Sentence 1 establishes that Kepler-1552b is potentially habitable but is unreachable (2,507 light years away). Sentence 2 pivots to an alternative action humanity must take instead. The second sentence does not add to the first, exemplify it, or follow it in sequence — it replaces an impractical option with a practical one.

steps.title: Match the relationship to the transition — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
• Choice A — "For example,": signals that sentence 2 gives a specific instance of a general claim made in sentence 1. But sentence 2 is not an example of Kepler-1552b's conditions — it describes a completely different action. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — "Likewise,": signals similarity or addition. There is no similarity between focusing on a distant planet and making nearby planets habitable; the relationship is substitution, not parallel addition. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — "Instead,": signals that humanity should do one thing in place of another — precisely the substitution relationship here. Correct.
  • Choice D — "Next,": signals a sequential step following the first. The passage does not suggest these are sequential steps; the second option replaces the first because the first is impossible. Eliminated.
